<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating profile screen**

I'm replacing the placeholder in ProfileScreen with the full layout to match other tabs. I'm removing unnecessary data fetching hooks from ProfileScreen since static profile data is sufficient. 

I'm adding a small test for a formatter function in profileFormatters.test.ts since the profile screen is display-only but has pure functions that can be tested. I'm extracting ProfileHeader as a local component and adding accessibility labels. </details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Confirming: `ProfileScreen.tsx` is wired in the bottom tab but is a minimal placeholder. Other main tabs use `GlobalAppBar` and a matching safe-area pattern; Profile does not. The repo doesn't include a `superpowers` skill file in the workspace (references point to `~/.config/superpowers/skills/`), so we'll use a superpowers-style plan: clear acceptance criteria, a thin vertical slice, and tests only where we add testable logic.



_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Profile screen UI",
  "overview": "Replace the placeholder Profile tab with a real screen that matches the shell and design tokens used by Home, Search, and Watchlist: same app bar, scrollable content, and theme (colors, spacing, typography). Content stays app-local (no TMDB user session) unless you later add auth.",
  "plan": "# Profile screen (StreamList)\n\n## Current state\n\n- The Profile tab is already registered in [&#96;streamlist/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](streamlist/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx) and typed in [&#96;streamlist/src/navigation/types.ts&#96;](streamlist/src/navigation/types.ts).\n- [&#96;streamlist/src/screens/ProfileScreen.tsx&#96;](streamlist/src/screens/ProfileScreen.tsx) is only a centered \"Profile\" label, uses &#96;ScreenErrorBoundary&#96;, and does **not** use [&#96;streamlist/src/components/common/GlobalAppBar.tsx&#96;](streamlist/src/components/common/GlobalAppBar.tsx), unlike the other main tabs.\n- The project has **no** TMDB account APIs in [&#96;streamlist/src/api/&#96;](streamlist) and no user-auth flow, so a \"logged-in TMDB profile\" would be a much larger product change (OAuth, token storage, account endpoints). This plan targets an **in-app / local profile** that fits the existing architecture.\n\n## Design alignment (reuse existing patterns)\n\nFollow the same **screen shell** as [&#96;streamlist/src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx&#96;](streamlist/src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx) (lines 570–581):\n\n- Outer &#96;View&#96; with &#96;screenWrap&#96; + &#96;backgroundColor: colors.surface&#96;.\n- &#96;GlobalAppBar&#96; (logo + notification affordance) at the top.\n- &#96;SafeAreaView&#96; with &#96;edges={['left', 'right']}&#96; only (app bar already handles the top safe area).\n- Inner &#96;ScrollView&#96; for vertical content with &#96;showsVerticalScrollIndicator={false}&#96; and &#96;contentContainerStyle&#96; that uses &#96;spacing&#96; tokens (e.g. bottom &#96;spacing.xl&#96;, vertical gaps &#96;spacing.lg&#96; / &#96;spacing.md&#96;).\n\nStyling rules from [&#96;.cursor/rules/streamlist.mdc&#96;](streamlist/.cursor/rules/streamlist.mdc) and the codebase:\n\n- All colors from [&#96;streamlist/src/theme/colors.ts&#96;](streamlist/src/theme/colors.ts) (e.g. &#96;surface&#96;, &#96;surface_container_high&#96; for “cards” / grouped rows, &#96;on_surface&#96; / &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; for text hierarchy, &#96;primary_container&#96; for accents; **no** new hex in components).\n- All spacing from [&#96;streamlist/src/theme/spacing.ts&#96;](streamlist/src/theme/spacing.ts) (no raw pixel values in &#96;StyleSheet&#96;).\n- Typography: reuse &#96;typography.textStyle&#96; patterns already used on Watchlist (e.g. &#96;labelSm&#96; for small uppercase labels, &#96;bodyMd&#96; / &#96;titleSm&#96; for body) — see [&#96;streamlist/src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx&#96;](streamlist/src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx) title block styles (&#96;collectionLabel&#96;, &#96;screenTitle&#96;).\n\n**Icons:** Match existing usage — &#96;react-native-vector-icons&#96; (&#96;Ionicons&#96; in navigation; &#96;MaterialIcons&#96; on other screens). Pick a small set of icons (e.g. &#96;bookmark&#96; for watchlist, &#96;open-outline&#96; for external link) and keep sizes derived from &#96;spacing&#96; where possible.\n\n**Interaction:** &#96;Pressable&#96; for tappable rows with &#96;style={({ pressed }) =\u003e [base, pressed \u0026\u0026 styles.pressed]}&#96; and use &#96;colors.surface_bright&#96; for the pressed state on dark surfaces (per project rule).\n\n## Proposed content (concrete, shippable default)\n\n1. **Header block** (same information hierarchy as other screens): a screen title and short subtitle in &#96;on_surface&#96; / &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; (no new navigation params).\n2. **Your list (local stats):** one compact card showing **watchlist size** from [&#96;streamlist/src/store/watchlistStore.ts&#96;](streamlist/src/store/watchlistStore.ts) (&#96;entries.length&#96;) — reuses Zustand only, no new API hook. This satisfies “profile” utility without auth.\n3. **About the app:** app display name and version string. There is no existing runtime version constant in the repo ([&#96;app.json&#96;](streamlist/app.json) has no &#96;version&#96;); the smallest approach is a single &#96;APP_VERSION&#96; / &#96;APP_DISPLAY_NAME&#96; in a small module under &#96;src/constants/&#96; (values aligned with &#96;package.json&#96; 0.0.1) so components stay free of ad hoc literals.\n4. **TMDB attribution (recommended):** short legal/attribution text and an optional tappable row that opens the TMDB website via React Native’s built-in [&#96;Linking.openURL&#96;](https://reactnative.dev/docs/linking) (no new dependencies). Use a standard TMDB API attribution line (wording as required by [TMDB API terms](https://developer.themoviedb.org/docs/attributions)) — do not paraphrase into something inaccurate.\n\n**Out of scope for this slice (unless you explicitly want it next):** TMDB account login, editable user fields, avatars, push settings, and network-backed profile hooks.\n\n## Architecture note (hooks rule)\n\nThe rule “custom hook for every data fetch per screen” applies to **API** work ([&#96;streamlist/.cursor/rules/streamlist.mdc&#96;](streamlist/.cursor/rules/streamlist.mdc)). A profile that only composes Zustand selectors + constants + &#96;Linking&#96; does **not** need a &#96;useXxx&#96; data-fetching hook. If you later add TMDB &#96;GET /3/account&#96; or config fetch, add &#96;useQueryResult&#96;-shaped hook + client method.\n\n## Test strategy (minimal, valuable)\n\n- If you extract any **pure** helpers (e.g. formatting a count, building attribution string), add a small unit test under &#96;streamlist/__tests__/&#96; (mirrors existing tests like [&#96;streamlist/__tests__/movieCard.test.ts&#96;](streamlist/__tests__/movieCard.test.ts)).\n- Full-screen snapshot tests are optional; the project’s tests lean toward logic and small components.\n\n## Files to touch\n\n| File | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| [&#96;streamlist/src/screens/ProfileScreen.tsx&#96;](streamlist/src/screens/ProfileScreen.tsx) | Real layout, scroll content, Zustand stats, &#96;Linking&#96;, &#96;ScreenErrorBoundary&#96; + no-op/aggregate &#96;onRetry&#96; if you keep the boundary. |\n| New: &#96;streamlist/src/constants/appInfo.ts&#96; (or similar) | &#96;APP_DISPLAY_NAME&#96;, &#96;APP_VERSION&#96; (single place for “About”). |\n| [&#96;streamlist/__tests__/...&#96;](streamlist/__tests__/) | Only if a testable pure helper is added. |\n\n**No** navigation or dependency changes for the default scope.\n\n## Visual structure (mermaid)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TB\n  subgraph screen [ProfileScreen]\n    G[GlobalAppBar]\n    SA[SafeAreaView left right]\n    SC[ScrollView]\n    H[Title section]\n    W[Watchlist stats card]\n    A[About app card]\n    T[TMDB attribution + link row]\n  end\n  G --\u003e SA\n  SA --\u003e SC\n  SC --\u003e H\n  SC --\u003e W\n  SC --\u003e A\n  SC --\u003e T\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## “Superpowers”-style planning (no skill file in repo)\n\nThe training workspace does not include a &#96;superpowers&#96; &#96;SKILL.md&#96; on disk. This plan uses the same **slice-based** idea: one vertical deliverable (Profile tab UI + tokens + local stats + attribution), clear acceptance checks below, and tests only for extracted pure logic so you can iterate without scope creep.\n\n### Acceptance checks\n\n- Profile tab matches the **same top structure** as Home/Search/Watchlist (app bar + scrollable body).\n- **No** raw hex or ad hoc spacing in &#96;StyleSheet&#96; (only &#96;colors&#96; + &#96;spacing&#96; + &#96;typography&#96;).\n- **Watchlist** count updates when the store changes (Zustand subscription in the screen).\n- TMDB link (if present) only opens public URLs; handle &#96;Linking.canOpenURL&#96; failure with an inline &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; message (no silent failure).\n- &#96;ScreenErrorBoundary&#96; remains; &#96;onRetry&#96; can remain a no-op for static content or re-read store if you add more dynamic pieces later.\n",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Match Watchlist screen shell: screenWrap, GlobalAppBar, SafeAreaView, ScrollView + ScreenErrorBoundary",
      "id": "shell",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Add header, watchlist count (Zustand), about (appInfo constants), TMDB attribution + Linking row with Pressable states",
      "id": "content",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Style with colors/spacing/typography only; surface_container_* cards; no dividers; surface_bright on press",
      "id": "style",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Add __tests__ only for any new pure formatters; run lint/jest for touched files",
      "id": "tests",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

</tool-use>
